paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRASG12D inhibitor
hypothesis: MRTX1133, a high-affinity non-covalent small molecule, can selectively inhibit KRASG12D mutant protein and demonstrate anti-tumor activity in KRASG12D-driven cancers

stages.description: Predict 3D structures of KRAS variants (WT and G12D mutant) to validate structural differences

jobs.description: Predict structure of KRAS G12D mutant protein
validates_claims: KRASG12D mutations show structural differences enabling selective targeting

jobs.description: Predict structure of KRAS wild-type protein
validates_claims: KRASG12D mutations show structural differences enabling selective targeting

stages.description: Dock MRTX1133 to both KRAS G12D and WT structures to validate selectivity

jobs.description: Dock MRTX1133 to KRAS G12D structure
validates_claims: MRTX1133 demonstrates high-affinity binding to KRASG12D
KRASG12D mutations show higher binding affinity to MRTX1133 compared to KRASWT

claims.claim: MRTX1133 demonstrates high-affinity binding to KRASG12D with KD value of ~0.2 pM for GDP-loaded KRASG12D
MRTX1133 shows ~700-fold selectivity for KRASG12D over KRASWT
MRTX1133 binding results in conformational change of switch I and switch II loops
MRTX1133 potently binds both active and inactive forms of KRASG12D
MRTX1133 inhibits ERK1/2 phosphorylation with median IC50 values of ~5 nM in KRASG12D cell lines
MRTX1133 demonstrates >1,000-fold selectivity in cell viability assays comparing KRASG12D vs KRASWT cell lines
MRTX1133 achieves tumor regression (≥30%) in 73% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma models
MRTX1133 binding eliminates KRASG12D protein surface competency for binding effector proteins
